title: plugsuit construction
wikitext:
[]: plugsuit construction             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Dec 10, 2014 9:15 pm [Split from the pic thread.] ChamucaRobota wrote: Her expression is a bit creepy, though... Taking off the plugsuit is so liberating as unhooking your bra?

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Wed Dec 10, 2014 10:25 pm ChamucaRobota wrote: Taking off the plugsuit is so liberating as unhooking your bra? Personally, I love when artists put a bike with Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Dec 10, 2014 10:52 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: Think of it like a skintight, all-body bra.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Dec 10, 2014 10:54 pm Chuckman wrote: We have already determined that plugsuits do not confer that level of support.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Dec 10, 2014 11:01 pm Asuka likes to wear hers around so it can't be that bad. Or am I misremembering? Doesn't she hang out in one at some point or am I just thinking about her head things? I would expect they're either surprisingly comfortable or not designed for the pilot's physical comfort at all and thus a pain in the ass to wear for extended periods. I would think it's the former, though. Wouldn't a distraction contradict their supposed purpose of enhancing synchronization? Also we need to consider the possibility that they are rock solid (despite having the suit that appears to have the least amount of support, Rei's boobs don't bounce that often) and Mari's boobs are just abnormally dense and heavy.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Dec 10, 2014 11:09 pm I can't recall the pilots ever wearing their plugsuits at a time that wasn't directly before, during, or directly after an Evangelion sortie. As to the comfort of the plugsuits, I agree that they're probably not bad-- but being inside any skintight garment for long enough inside a tube filled with liquid is going to get kind of uncomfortable. It's the sort of thing that makes you wonder if the pilots get prune fingers after taking their suits off, or if they ever have moisture-related problems eg. athlete's goot. I doubt that they're rock solid since we see how easily they can fold. Thick, probably-- but not solid. I think we can also assume that Rei and Asuka, despite being bitterly locked in a battle to determine who has the bigger boobies, both pale in comparison to Mari.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Dec 10, 2014 11:12 pm Parts of them are clearly something other than the super-flexible material. Rei's suit appears to have a shaped chest piece built in while Asuka's has more traditional cups but they're a funny shape and not attached to anything so there's no reason why they wouldn't just flop around uselessly. They also have whatever is in the backpack part and a built in ability to defibrillate the pilots so there's no reason to assume that they wouldn't have sports-bra like padding built in and something like an athletic cup for Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Dec 10, 2014 11:15 pm That would be the thick parts, yeah. I wasn't really thinking about the back unit when I was typing, but I'd guess that it's made out of some kind of thick plastic. Maybe bakelite, since it's used for a ton of other things in the Evaverse. While they're flexible, we can hear them creak as the pilots make more complex movements, so it stands to reason that they're a thicker kind of latex, which would be suited pretty well to stop breasts from jostling around too much.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Dec 10, 2014 11:18 pm Considering how they work I've always assumed they're made of some kind of memory cloth, like Batman's cape. The wrist switch controls an electrical current that returns them to a programmed shape for the pilot's body, which is why Shinji found Asuka's suit uncomfortable. Clearly that also means that at some point post-Q the backup batteries in the pilot's suits will wear out and they will go baggy and they'll all have to strip and carry them.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Dec 10, 2014 11:27 pm More proof that, as I have always said, great things flow from breasts.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Dec 10, 2014 11:59 pm I just dread how much they would ride up in the crotch & ass.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Dec 11, 2014 12:00 am If we consider that the plugsuits might be made out of a thicker latex-ish material, riding up wouldn't be much of a problem.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Dec 11, 2014 3:55 am Well Asuka and Mari's second plugsuits in Q definitively ride up!

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Thu Dec 11, 2014 4:03 am So this is a thread. I would think that the fact that they are skintight and you need to be naked while wearing them kinda implies they would ride up.

[]:             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Thu Dec 11, 2014 4:04 am I've always imagined that they work like those vacuum bags that are used to store food for long periods of time, and that the somewhat bulky unit on the back is the vacuum system itself. The button on the wrist would activate the vacuum unit and suck all the air out of the suit.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Dec 11, 2014 4:29 am They do suck the air out of the suit: you can see their a gust of wind coming from the collar overture and ruffling the pilot hair when he activate the suit. As for why they have to wear it naked, it's stated that somehow they facilitate the synchronization (episode 1, Rits comments that Shinji managed a 40% without even a plugsuit), so the plugsuit certainly have many in-built systems to assist in the synchronization. Finally, life support system such as a defibrillator work better when there isn't clothes between the device and skin.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Dec 11, 2014 4:49 am Although, that would be air forced out of the collar, implying that the suit seals asymmetrically in such a way to catch air pockets at the upper part of the body (that don't get forced out through the unit on their backs.)

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Thu Dec 11, 2014 6:46 am Chuckman wrote: We have already determined that plugsuits do not confer that level of support.

[]:             Quote Post by LukeMM95 » Fri Dec 12, 2014 7:14 am Gendo'sPapa wrote: I just dread how much they would ride up in the crotch & ass.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Dec 12, 2014 12:27 pm For the guys, obviously an appropriate amount. Not so much for the girls, though-- as demonstrated by Shinji's uncomfortableness in episode 8. Either that or Shinji and Toji just have small wingdigglers.
summary:
[]: plugsuit construction             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Dec 10, 2014 9:15 pm [Split from the pic thread.] ChamucaRobota wrote: Her expression is a bit creepy, though... Taking off the plugsuit is so liberating as unhooking your bra?

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Wed Dec 10, 2014 10:25 pm ChamucaRobota wrote: Taking off the plugsuit is so liberating as unhookin
